spell_descriptions: "[h1]Physician-Only Spell Descriptions[/h1]
[br]
[row]
[col]
[h2]Bolster[/h2]
[i]Evocation cantrip[/i][br]
[b]Casting Time:[/b] 1 action[br]
[b]Range:[/b] Touch[br]
[b]Components:[/b] V, S[br]
[b]Duration:[/b] Instantaneous

You reach out and speed up a creature's natural healing. If the target has at least 1 hitpoint, it can spend a hit die and regain that many hit points, along with an additional 1d4 hit points. If the creature has no hit dice remaining, this spell has no effect.

This spell's extra healing inereases by 1d4 when you reach 5th level (2d4), 11th level (3d4), and 17th level (4d4).
[/col]        
[col]
[h2]Analgesic Veil[/h2]
[i]4th level conjuration[/i][br]
[b]Casting Time:[/b] 1 action[br]
[b]Range:[/b] Self (15 foot radius sphere)[br]
[b]Components:[/b] V, M, S (a clear mirror worth at least 200 gp)[br]
[b]Duration:[/b] 1 minute

An aurora of warding energy pours out from you, suffusing a 15 ft. radius area around you. You and all allied creatures
within that area roll three of their hit dice and gain that amount in temporary hit points. These hit points last until they are lost, or until one minute passes. These hit dice are only used for calculation and are not spent. Creatures affected by the spell become covered in a shimmering layer of magical energy that represents this spell.

[b]At Higher Levels.[/b] When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, add one additional hit die to the roll for each slot level above 4th.
[/col]
[/row]
"
class_features: "[h1]Succor[/h1]
You possess within you the ability to increase the potency of your healing magic. At 1st level, you gain the ability to increase the amount of hit points a single spell can restore. By expending one use of Succor, you can treat a spell that restores hit points as though you cast it a level higher than normal. This effect can increase up to a number equal to your  charisma modifier (minimum of 1). For example, if you use 2 uses of Succor when casting a 1st level spell, you can cast it as though it were 3rd level. 

You have 2 uses of Succor before a long rest. The number of uses you have before a long rest increases to 4 at 5th level, 6 at 11th level, and 8 at 17th level.

[h1]Healer's Art[/h1]
When you reach 2nd level, you choose a method of refining your medical skill . There are three main arts, Restoration, Creation, and Purification, and two non-traditional arts, Enhancement and Reinforcement. These are all detailed at the end of the class description.

Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level.

[h1]Adrenal Infusion[/h1]
Starting at 7th level, you can use your Physic to directly infuse your allies with supernatural vim and vigor. As an action, you can spend one use of Succor to grant the following effects to a willing creature you can see:
[ul]
[li]The creature can take one additional action on top of of their regular action or possible bonus action.
[li]They have resistances to non-magical weapon damage. Their movement speed is increased by 10 feet.
[li]They have advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution checks and saving throws.
[/ul]
These effects disappear after one minute. This feature has no effect on undead or constructs.

[h1]Preceptor of Physic[/h1]
Starting at 20th level, your internal connection to Physic has become an unconscious fixture of your very spirit. Whenever you cast a spell that restores hit points, you treat it as though it had a single use of Succor already applied to it. This works with any Healer's Art features that use Succor as a trigger. In addition, you regain 1 use of succor whenever you finish a short rest.
"
subclass_options: "[h1] Healer's Arts [/h1]
Since the first discovery of Physic, physicians have been making great strides in the development and application of medical thaumaturgy. In the numerous Grand Academies that dot the Realms, Physic is studied rigorously and thoroughly by physicians ever ready to improve their craft.

The (legal) applications of Physic are split up into three main studies (Restoration, Creation, Purification) known collectively as the "Healer's Arts". 

Countless physicians have devoted the entirety of their lives to the refinement of these arts. In certain areas, one Art may dominate the entire medical field, with a stigma in place towards the others. Elsewhere, the three Arts may coexist in harmony, thus raising the quality of life in that region. Even in isolation, a would-be physician may find some composition that belongs to an Art and thus awakens herself to the practice. Since each Art requires a different manner of attuning oneself to the Physic, it is entirely possible to begin to learn an Art completely by accident. Though, of course, it is generally only legal to practice these Arts with a license issued by one of the Grand Academies.

The fourth art, Enhancement, is strictly illegal to practice by licensed Physicians, but that doesn't stop it from being practiced in the seedy outskirts of society. The Art of Reinforcement, while officially frowned upon, is nevertheless recognized as a necessity for certain dangerous regions.

[spoiler] 
The Art of Restoration is the most straightforward of the Healer's Arts. Even though it lacks the versatility of other Arts, it makes up for it its honest dedication to healing the injured. When the common folk think of physicians, they generally think of those that follow the Art of Restoration. Called 'menders,' these Physicians enhance their application of Physic with energy from the Positive plane. Wherever there might be some terrible catastrophe, and thus an influx of wounded, it is good to hope that there is a mender nearby. As one might expect, their emotional catalysts tend to focus on pity and compassion for the wounded.

[h3] Positive Connection [/h3]
Beginning when you select this art at 2nd level, your learn to bolster your magic with energy from the Positive Plane. As a result, you gain resistance to necrotic damage and attacks that lower your maximum hit points.

[h3] Convalescence [/h3]
Starting at 2nd level, your suffusion in positive energy has a noticeable effect on natural recovery. When your or an ally within 50 feet of you roll hit dice, you may choose to roll it again and take the higher amount of hit points regained. In addition, during a short or long rest, you and your allies can roll one of their hit dice without spending it.

[h3] Respected Practice [/h3]
Starting at 6th level, whenever a sentient creature has seen you restore hit points to another, you gain advantage on Persuasion checks as long as they are not hostile to you. This advantage lasts until it is used.

[h3] Blooming Health [/h3]
At 10th level, your learn to improve someone's well-being even after they are as healthy as possible. Whenever you restore hit points to a creature and the amount healed exceeds their maximum hit points, the remaining hit points are given to that creature as temporary hit points. These temporary hit points last for ten minutes or until replaced or exhausted.

[h3] Solicitude [/h3]
Starting at 14th level, you share in the health you give to your allies. Whenever you would restore hit points to a creature with a spell that has Succor applied to it, half of that amount is restored to you. If you are restoring health to multiple allies, you gain half of the health given to a single ally, not the cumulative amount given to all. These hit points function with the Blooming Health Art feature.

[h3] Myriad Benedictions [/h3]
At 18th level, your expertise in healing allows for your blessings to multiply before your eyes. When you cast a spell that restores hit dice and requires a dice roll, you can reroll any die that displays its maximum value and add that value to the total. There is no limit to how many dice you can reroll in this way, and a single die can be rerolled multiple times so long as its maximum is rolled each time.
|[b]Art of Restoration[/b] [/spoiler]

[spoiler]
Those that follow the Art of Creation see Physic differently than students of other Arts. While others see it as a lifeimproving force to be used for healing others, students of the Art of Creation see it as a source of life that can be used to summon creatures that could aid a physician in her noble practice. After all, are not two physicians better than one? Called 'channelers', these Physicians enhance their application of physic with the artful sculpting of familiars that are capable of healing alongside their masters. Theirs is the Art that most directly characterizes Physicians as artisans of life. This ambition, pride, and creativity serves as their catalysts. In hospices, it is a good idea to have at least one channeler in employ, as they provide a convenient way for checking on multiple patients at once. 

[h3] Therapeutic Familiar [/h3]
Beginning when you select this art at 2nd level, you learn the Find Familiar spell and can cast it as a ritual. When you cast the spell, you can choose one of the following forms for your familiar: non-venomous snake, psuedodragon, sprite, bat, cat, owl, lizard, fish, rat, raven, sea horse, weasel, or fox. Your familiar has the statistics of the chosen form, though it is fey instead of whatever it would be normally. When summoned, your familiar learns the following cantrips: Bolster, Guidance, and Light. It is capable of using these cantrips on its own turn, though it uses your spellcasting modifier and level when it does so. 

[h3] Innate Connection [/h3]
As your closest ally and most direct expression of yourself, your familiar grows as you do. Your familiar uses your proficiency bonus instead of its own. It also adds your Charisma modifier to its AC. For each level you gain after your 2nd level, your familiar gains an additional hit die and increases its hit points accordingly. Should you choose to replace your familiar, the replacement has the same number of increased hit dice.

[h3] Physician's Assistant [/h3]
Starting at 6th level, your familiar begins unconsciously learning your mannerisms and habits as a physician. When you perform any non-standard actions to heal someone, say by performing a surgery or by dressing infected wounds, your familiar will automatically use the Aid action to give you advantage on your Medicine checks. This can only be used, of course, when your familiar is present and available to help you. 

[container:box-25]
[table]
[tr][th]Physician[br]Level[/th][th][br]1st[/th][th][br]2nd[/th][th][br]3rd[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]10[/td][td]2[/td][td]1[/td][td]0[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]14[/td][td]2[/td][td]2[/td][td]1[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]18[/td][td]3[/td][td]2[/td][td]2[/td][/tr][/table][/container]
[h3] Disciple of Medicine [/h3]
Starting at 10th level, your familiar forms a direct connection of its own to the Source of Physic You are able to teach it some of the spells you yourself know, which it will be able to cast exactly as you would. Whenever you take a long rest, you can teach your familiar a number of spells equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). The familiar must have the spell slots to cast it and you must know the spell yourself to be able to teach it. You are allowed to change these spells every time you take a long rest. Whenever you gain a new familiar, you must teach it spells once more. While the number of spells you can teach does not normally change by level, the amount of spell slots your familiar has does change. Your familiar cannot use your stores of Succor unless you cast a spell through it as decribed by the Find Familiar spell.

[h3] Dissipation [/h3]
Starting at 14th level, your familiar becomes more sympathetic to your work as a physician and actually becomes willing to sacrifice itself for your cause. As an action, you can temporarily sever your connection with your familiar, causing them to instantly disappear. When this hnappens, you gain three uses of Succor and the spell slots the Familiar had in reserve. Upon using this feature you will not be able to cast Find Familiar for 24 hours as your connection to your former familiar slowly and painfully dissolves. The next time you cast Find Familiar you will be able to call back your previous familiar instead of creating a new one. You can still locate a new familiar if you wish. 

[h3] Rousing Renewal [/h3]
At 18th level, your familiar can learn to draw upon physic to heal itself. All it needs is a little help from you. As a bonus action, you can spend a use of Succor to restore up to half of your familiar's hit points at once. The influx of restorative energy also restores 3 cumulative levels' worth of your familiar's spell slots. For 1 minute afterwards, your familiar will be able to cast spells with a casting time of 1 action as a bonus action. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so until after you complete a long rest. 
| [b]Art of Creation[/b] [/spoiler]

[spoiler]
Students of the Art of Purification are often seen as the more "eccentric" of physicians. While other Physicians might see diseases as nothing more than something to be cured, students of this Art see a certain merit in emulating them in some regard. To them, diseases are another facet of life, though one that may hold too much sway in an individual than should be necessary. The name is both an irony and a truth: while they view diseases as something that can strengthen a person, and thus may intentionally induce it, they also are the best suited to purifying it. Students of this art are the least likely to share emotional catalysts. Called 'cleansers', these Physicians use Physic to manipulate the internal energies of foes and allies alike. They can use their spells to heal just as well as they can use them to inflict diseases on their foes. Cleansers stride into plagueinfested areas, ready to make it their crowning achievement. 

[h3] Chain Heal [/h3]
Beginning when you select this art at 2nd level, your healing is not limited to the individual you are actually treating. When you cast a spell that restores health points to a creature, any friendly creatures within 5 feet of them gain the spell level + your Charisma modifier in HP as well. This also works with spells that restore hit points to multiple creatures at once. This does not work on cantrips. A creature can only benefit from repeat application of this feature twice. This means that if you cast a spell that restores hit points to multiple creatures and the creature in question is in range of more than 2 other creatures, they only gain the aforementioned amount twice instead of however many they times they would otherwise. 

[h3] Fortifying Exposure [/h3]
Starting at 2nd level, you gain the ability to make your allies stronger through 'experience.' When an allied creature within your line of sight takes damage, you can use your reaction and a use of Succor to give that creature resistance to the type of damage it recieved. This resistance lasts until the end of your next turn. At 16th level, you give the creature immunity to that damage type instead. 

[h3] Inoculation [/h3]
At 6th level, your deepened connection enhances your natural resistances. Whenever you are exposed to diseases, or toxins that cause the "Sleep" or "Poisoned" status effects, you gain advantage on the Constitution saving throw to avoid it. If the disease is created by a spell, such as Contagion, this does not apply. 

[h3] Vaccination [/h3]
Starting at 10th level, your healing no longer simply affects the body, but it also affects all other aspects of an individual. Whenever you apply a use of Succor to a spell that restores hit points to a creature, if that creature is Paralyzed, Deafened, Poisoned, Diseased, or Blinded, it can reroll its Saving Throw. If it succeeds on the saving throw, it becomes immune to any of the above conditions for a minute. If the creature is not afflicted with any of the above status effects, it gains advantage on Constitution Saving Throws to resist those status effects instead, also for a minute. 

[h3] Healing Dew / Miasma [/h3]
At 14th level, you discover a method to further diffuse your magic. You learn the Aura of Life and Aura of Purity spells from the Paladin spell list. These spells count as Physician spells for you and use your spellcasting modifier as a result. If you cast Aura of Purity with a spell slot of 5th level or above and a use of Succor, you can cast Contagion on any unallied creatures in that radius, expending a use of Succor or a spell slot of 4th level or above for each new target. 

[h3] Hygienic Touch [/h3]
Starting at 18th level, your intimacy with disease has given you an uncanny ability to prevent it. You can cast Purify Food and Drink, Lesser Restoration and Detect Poison and Disease without spending a spell slot or material components. In addition, any surfaces within 15 feet of you that are covered in dust, grime, oil, or other detritus are automatically cleaned within 1 turn. This includes people. You may choose to activate or deactivate this aura with a bonus action.
|[b]Art of Purification[/b] [/spoiler]

[spoiler]
While the Art of Purification may be viewed with mild disdain, the Art of Enhancement is treated with outright scorn. To traditionalists, the Art of Enhancement is no Art at all, but a bastardization of everything a Physician should hold dear. Students of this school use their Physic to improve their combat prowess, though whether or not they do this to help others is up to the Physician in question. Called 'war-surgeons' at best and 'quacks' at worst, these Physicians treat their enemies as diseases to be cured or tumors to be excised. Their emotional connection to Physic is most characterized by fanaticism or boisterous confidence. Most students of this Art are beholden to no school. In fact, Enhancement is the second most common art to develop spontaneously, with Restoration being the most common. 

[h3] Operating Theatre of War [/h3]
Beginning when you select this art at 2nd level, you gain proficiency with light armor and with one type of melee weapon of your choice. 

[h3] Doping [/h3]
Starting at 2nd level, you develop a forbidden method of supplementing your performance. Provided that you aren't wearing medium or heavy armor, or equipped with a shield, you are able to use Succor to improve your physical parameters. You can use your bonus action and a use of Succor to start doping for the next minute. This effect ends early if you don medium or heavy armor, or if you choose to dismiss it. Ending it early does not require an action. While you are doping, you gain the following effects: You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift. You suspend the effects of Exhaustion for the duration of this feature. You gain a bonus to any Constitution saving throws you make to maintain concentration on a spell. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1). 

[h3] Extra Attack [/h3]
At 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. 

[h3] Bloodbath [/h3]
Starting at 10th level, the Succor you infuse during your doping starts to react with the flow of battle. When you make a successful melee attack against a creature while doping, you regain a fourth of the damage dealt in hit points (rounded up). 

[h3] Between Life and Death [/h3]
At 14th level, your doping nullifies your sense of pain, allowing you to ignore otherwise fatal wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while doping and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 hit point instead. Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a short or long rest, the DC resets to 10. 

[h3] Physician-Assisted Homicide [/h3]
Starting at 18th level, you refuse to let anything come between you and a new target. When you bring a creature to 0 hit points while doping, you can choose another creature within 30 feet. As a bonus action, you can make an attack roll against them. If you are successful, you deal damage as normal and appear in the closest unoccupied space to them. Moving in this way ignores attacks of opportunity and difficult terrain.
| [b]Art of Enhancement[/b] [/spoiler]

[spoiler]
The Art of Reinforcement takes the selflessness often attributed to Physicians and internalizes it into the very flesh of its practitioners. It is their calling to prevent injuries, whether it is by intervening in combat or by self-sacrifice. This is one of the more recently developed arts and as such is viewed with apprehension by the central arts. Still, they cannot help but laud its effectiveness, even if they don't appreciate its focus on war. Called 'sentinels,' these Physicians turn the magic used for revitalizing onto their own bodies. With this, they create minute electrical charges that steel their flesh, allowing them to repel even the strongest of blows. They invoke Physic with a variety of feelings. Vengeance may fuel it perhaps, but so may compassion. While the Art has a singular purpose, its adherents can be as different as night and day. 

[h3] Unarmored Defense [/h3]
Beginning when you select this art at 2nd level, your body naturally becomes tougher. While you are not wearing any armor, your base armor class is 14 + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit. In addition, you gain resistance to lightning damage. 

[h3] Galvanize [/h3]
Starting at 2nd level, you learn to mitigate damage by muscle flexion alone. When you take damage, you can expend one spell slot to reduce the damage actually taken. For a first level spell slot, roll 1d10 and add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled. Reduce the damage by that total. For each spell slot above 1st, roll an additional 1d10. Making use of this feature does not use your reaction. Succor may be used to increase the effective level of the spell slot used. 

[h3] Sympathetic Aura [/h3]
At 6th level, you can protect your allies from injuries and ills, at the cost of your own well-being. As a reaction when an ally within 15 feet of you takes damage, you instead magically take half of that damage, along with any effects the source of that damage might cause. You can choose to mitigate this damage with Galvanize if you wish. 

[h3] Improved Galvanize [/h3]
Starting at 10th level, your experience with the technique enables you to draw out power more reliably. If you roll a 1 or a 2 on any of your dice when using the Galvanize feature, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll. 

[h3] Reflective Impulse [/h3]
At 14th level, you learn to weaponize your protective technique. When you lower damage taken using the Galvanize feature, you can use your reaction to deal the amount you rolled back as Force damage. You must be able to see the source of the attack in order to make use of this feature. 

[h3] Defibrillation [/h3]
Starting at 18th level, you learn to use the same electric charges that power your Galvanism to resuscitate yourself in times of need. When you would make a death saving throw, you can instead spring back to your feet with a burst of rejuvinating lightning. You immediately stand up (if you so choose), and you regain hit points equal to half your hit point maximum. All hostile creatures within 30 feet of you take 15 + your Charisma Modifier in lightning damage, while all nonhostile creatures within that same area gain 20 + your Charisma modifier in hit points. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest. 
|[b]Art of Reinforcement[/b] [/spoiler]
"
feats: "If your DM allows the use of feats from chapter 6 of the Player’s Handbook, you can make use of the following feats.

[row]
[col]
[h2]Physician Neophyte[/h2]
[b]Prerequisite:[/b] The ability to cast at least one spell that restores hit points[br]
You awaken within yourself the beginnings of a connection to Physic, allowing you to improve your capacity to heal. 
[ul]
[li]Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20 
[li]You gain proficiency with an Herbalism kit
[li]You gain 1 use of Succor, which you can expend to treat a spell that restores hit points as though you cast it a single level higher. This use is restored when you take a long rest.
[/ul]
[/col]
[col]
[h2]Lifebringer[/h2]
[b]Prerequisite:[/b] The ability to cast at least one spell that restores hit points[br]
You discover a weakness in undeath. When you cast a spell that restores hit points, you can replace the normal number of targets with an equal number of undead. These undead must make a saving throw against your Spell Save DC. Should they fail the saving throw, they take damage equal to twice the number of hit points to be restored and have disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of their next turn. On a success, nothing happens.

Power Word Heal functions identically to Power Word Kill when cast on an undead creature when using this feat.
[/col]
[/row]"
multiclassing: "To multi-class as a Physician you must have a 13 in Charisma. Multi-classing as a Physician grants you proficiency with an Herbalism kit.
 
 "

blocks.title: Warlock
blocks.textualdata:
name: Warlock
overview: 'Warlocks are beings who make a pact that establishes a connection with another being vastly more powerful than them, also known as a patron. In order to attain a goal that is attainable with vast amounts of power, however the fate of Warlock after death is almost always tragic. Seeing as most Patrons take the Warlocks soul for their own purposes.'
hit_dice: 1d8
hit_points_at_1st_level: '8 + your Constitution modifier'
hit_points_at_higher_levels: '1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per warlock level after 1st'
armor_proficiencies: 'Light armor'
weapon_proficiencies: 'Simple weapons'
tools: ''
saving_throws: 'Wisdom, Charisma'
skills: 'Choose two from Arcana, Deception, History, Intimidation, Investigation, Nature, and Religion'
starting_equipment: "You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:
- a light crossbow and 20 bolts OR any simple weapon
- a component pouch OR an arcane focus
- a scholar's pack OR a dungeoneer's pack
- Leather armor, any simple weapon, and two daggers"
spellcasting: "[h1]Cantrips[/h1]
You know two cantrips of your choice from the warlock spell list. You learn additional warlock cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Warlock table.

[h1]Spell Slots[/h1]
The Warlock table shows how many spell slots you have. The table also shows what the level of those slots is; all of your spell slots are the same level. To cast one of your warlock spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a short or long rest.

For example, when you are 5th level, you have two 3rd-level spell slots. To cast the 1st-level spell Witch Bolt, you must spend one of those slots, and you cast it as a 3rd-level spell.

[h1]Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher[/h1]
At 1st level, you know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the warlock spell list.
 
The Spells Known column of the Warlock table shows when you learn more warlock spells of your choice of 1st level or higher. A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level. When you reach 6th level, for example, you learn a new warlock spell, which can be 1st, 2nd, or 3rd level.
 
Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the warlock spells you know and replace it with another spell from the warlock spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

[h1]Spellcasting Ability[/h1]
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your warlock spells, so you use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a warlock spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.
[br][br]
Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier
[br]
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier


[h1]Spellcasting Focus[/h1]
You can use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells."
